conditions: Closest 4 ft piling on right is sharp with clear texture. The 11 ft/14 ft pilings further back are visible but appear as hazy silhouettes with little detail, similar to the 15–20 ft guideline rather than the 25 ft reference where 30 ft pilings are discernible. No clear 30 ft pilings visible.

Closest 4ft piling at right is very sharp with clear texture. The 11ft/14ft pilings farther back are visible but somewhat hazy, with less distinct detail and more silhouette-like than in the 20–35 ft examples. The sandy bottom is visible immediately below but there is no clear view of the far (~30ft) pilings. This matches conditions where only the nearer mid pilings are reliably visible, suggesting around 15 ft visibility.

Closest 4 ft piling on right is sharp with texture, 11 ft piling also clear; 14 ft/30 ft pilings not distinctly visible, water becomes uniform haze past mid-ground. This corresponds to conditions where only the 11 ft piling is clearly visible.

Bottom is clearly visible. The nearer pilings (4 ft and 11 ft) have strong texture; the 14 ft piling on the left is present but somewhat hazy. The far 30 ft pilings are not distinctly visible as silhouettes. This matches conditions where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded, indicating about 15 ft visibility.

Closest and mid pilings are sharp with texture. The back-left/center pilings around 30 ft are visible but only as soft silhouettes, not sharp. Sandy bottom is visible but hazy. This matches the ~25 ft reference more than the 30–35 ft cases.
